text: Spike: evaluate references\\mattpocock-eng-skills and references\\atv-starterkit for patterns, skills, workflow surfaces, and capabilities that autoharness could incorporate to fill current gaps or augment existing functionality. Produce recommendations on what to adopt, adapt, or reject, including rationale, fit with the 10 primitives/capability-pack model, and any concrete follow-on work.

Harden verify_workspace.py to scan .autoharness/harness-manifest.yaml scalar fields (esp. autoharness_version) for unresolved {{...}} placeholders, mechanically enforcing install-harness Step 4.1 item 5. Currently the verifier only scans rendered artifacts[]; a literal {{AUTOHARNESS_VERSION}} could ship undetected. Follow-up from 054-F adversarial review.
Write a date/time stamp after each output to the window to enable operator to see duration between work output.

eval reviewer: parse_unified_diff misreads an added line whose content starts with '+++ ' as a new-file header (resets hunk state, drops remaining added/context lines). Fix by tracking the hunk header's declared new-line count (@@ +c,d @@) and stopping header detection once the hunk is exhausted. Source: code-review P3 on 056-S, src/autoharness/eval/reviewer.py:145-148.

eval CLI: _eval_command help detection scans ALL remaining tokens, so 'eval review --base help' short-circuits to usage and a git ref literally named 'help' cannot be used as --base/--head. Fix by only treating a leading token (rest[0]) as a help request. Source: code-review P3 on 056-S, src/autoharness/cli.py _eval_command.
